applicability_notes: Constant-tensor creation op; torch-level equivalent is torch.full. Frequently the head of a scalar-division idiom in model code: ttnn.full(shape, fill_value=c) -> ttnn.reciprocal -> ttnn.mul(x, ...) which at torch level is simply x / c — a candidate for replacement by a single eltwise scalar op rather than materializing the constant tensor. Also used to create [1,1] scalar tiles for timestep/sigma broadcast in diffusion schedulers, and as mask-value source tensors for ttnn.scatter.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: tiny scalar tile [1,1] or [1,1,1,1] (timestep / sigma_difference broadcast constants)
placement_observations.source: diffusion pipeline call sites: device=submesh_device if not traced else None — tensor created host-side when tracing, default DRAM interleaved on device otherwise

placement_observations.size_descriptor: full activation shape (sample.shape / tt_initial_latents.shape, e.g. latent tensor [B,C,H,W])
placement_observations.source: LMS/Euler scheduler call sites: ttnn.full(sample.shape, fill_value=value, dtype=ttnn.bfloat16, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT) feeding reciprocal+mul; no explicit memory_config => default DRAM interleaved

placement_observations.size_descriptor: small index-shaped tensor (batch, 1) used as scatter src (mask fill value)
placement_observations.source: scatter masking call site: ttnn.full(idx_rm.shape, mask_value, layout=ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT, device=work.device()); ROW_MAJOR only when use_row_major branch taken, otherwise TILE via to_layout
unit test: optional_tensor path verified to allocate no new buffer pages (in-place fill into existing device buffer)
